title: HUD Panel Content Rewrites
description: Rewrite all 4 non-Team panel contents: InboxPanel->ActionPanel, ProjectsPanel->OpsPanel, IntelPanel->DirectivePanel, WikiPanel->LogPanel. Add BookshelfPanel as furniture panel. Each panel redesigned around CEO workflow questions.

scope.in: ActionPanel (urgency tiers, decision cards), OpsPanel (KPI strip, goal health cards), DirectivePanel (pipeline hero, project cards, artifact trail), LogPanel (event feed with date separators), BookshelfPanel (lessons viewer in furniture), SidePanel.tsx routing, panelUtils.ts shared components
scope.out: Tab shell changes (done in hud-tab-shell-and-badges), new backend endpoints, game engine/canvas changes, TeamPanel redesign

dod.criterion: ActionPanel shows 3 urgency tiers (Urgent/Needs Attention/Info) with waiting sessions and directive completion gates
OpsPanel shows 2x2 KPI strip and goal health cards with project status drill-down
DirectivePanel shows pipeline stepper hero view, project cards with task progress, and directive history
LogPanel shows chronological event feed with date separators and All/Important filter
BookshelfPanel renders lesson files when bookshelf furniture is clicked
All panels use PARCHMENT theme and pixel-art styling from panelUtils.ts

tasks.title: LogPanel with Event Feed
tasks.user_scenario: Player opens Log tab and sees a chronological feed of company events with date separators and importance filtering

dod.criterion: LogPanel.tsx exists and renders event feed from store data
Events sorted chronologically with date separator headers
All/Important filter toggle works — Important shows only errors, completions, approvals
Each event has icon, timestamp, and description in pixel-art style
Empty state shows 'No events yet' message

tasks.title: ActionPanel with Urgency Tiers
tasks.user_scenario: Player opens Action tab and sees items sorted by urgency
tasks.scope: Rewrite InboxPanel.tsx as ActionPanel.tsx with three urgency tiers.

dod.criterion: ActionPanel.tsx exists with 3 collapsible urgency tier sections
Urgent tier shows error sessions and failed reviews with red styling
Needs Attention tier shows waiting-approval sessions and awaiting_completion directives
Each item shows agent name, context summary, and time since created
Empty state shows 'All clear — nothing needs your attention' message

tasks.title: OpsPanel with KPI Strip & Goal Health
tasks.user_scenario: Player opens Ops tab and sees company KPIs and goal health
tasks.scope: Rewrite ProjectsPanel.tsx as OpsPanel.tsx with KPI strip and goal health cards.

dod.criterion: OpsPanel.tsx exists with KPI strip showing 4 metrics
Goal health cards render with status color (green/yellow/red based on project health)
Each goal card expands to show projects with task progress bars
KPI values computed from store data (goals, features/projects, tasks)
Backlog count shown with link/expand to backlog items per goal

tasks.title: DirectivePanel with Pipeline Hero View
tasks.user_scenario: Player opens Directive tab and sees pipeline progress with project cards
tasks.scope: Rewrite IntelPanel.tsx as DirectivePanel.tsx with pipeline stepper hero.

dod.criterion: DirectivePanel.tsx exists with pipeline stepper hero for active directive
Pipeline steps show status (completed/active/pending) with visual indicators
Project cards below stepper show task completion progress
Elapsed time displays since directive started_at
Empty state shows 'No active directive' when directiveState is null

tasks.title: BookshelfPanel & Final Wiring
tasks.user_scenario: Player clicks bookshelf furniture and sees lesson files; old panels cleaned up
tasks.scope: BookshelfPanel as furniture panel, delete old panels, update routing.

dod.criterion: BookshelfPanel.tsx exists showing lesson files when bookshelf furniture clicked
SidePanel.tsx routes to ActionPanel, OpsPanel, DirectivePanel, LogPanel for tab content
Old panel files (InboxPanel, ProjectsPanel, IntelPanel, WikiPanel) deleted
App compiles with zero TypeScript errors and all panels render

sequencing_rationale: LogPanel first as it's the most independent. ActionPanel and OpsPanel next. DirectivePanel depends on pipeline pattern. BookshelfPanel last for cleanup.
